confidence_rationale: Canonical computational-theory paper assigning distinct uncertainty-computation roles to NE (UNEXPECTED uncertainty from context-switches) and ACh (EXPECTED uncertainty from known cue unreliability). Indirectly supports Q-045's substrate-distinct verdict: NE is doing something computationally specific (unexpected-uncertainty signalling) that is qualitatively different from a recency-weighted anti-recency penalty. Supports a model where MECH-313's LC-tonic noise floor is a specific computational primitive, not a generic noise generator. Confidence moderate (0.74): canonical and heavily cited theoretical synthesis, but theoretical rather than empirical, and Yu-Dayan's NE role is unexpected-uncertainty rather than tonic-noise-floor specifically -- MECH-313 lives in a slightly different functional niche than the Yu-Dayan NE role.
failure_signatures: If REE implements MECH-313 as generic state-independent Gaussian noise on policy logits (the simplest noise-floor instantiation), it will not reproduce the unexpected-uncertainty signalling that Yu & Dayan attribute to NE -- a noise floor cannot detect context switches, which is what NE's unexpected-uncertainty role requires. The current MECH-313 substrate is closer to the noise-floor framing than to the unexpected-uncertainty framing. This may be an under-specification of MECH-313 that the Q-045 4-arm ablation surfaces: if 313-only fails to produce stochastic-mode-like behaviour under unbeatable-opponent conditions (Tervo's regime), it would be evidence that MECH-313's noise-floor primitive is too generic -- it needs an unexpected-uncertainty trigger.

If MECH-313 and MECH-260 are collapsed into one substrate (Q-045 collapse outcome), the combined regulator would have to instantiate BOTH the noradrenergic role (unexpected-uncertainty / mode-switching) AND the cingulate role (anti-recency / history-tracking). Yu & Dayan's framework treats these as computationally distinct -- they require different temporal kernels, different update rules, different gating conditions. Collapsing them produces an architecture that cannot reproduce the Yu-Dayan dissociation under simulated NE-vs-ACC pharmacology. Supports the substrate-distinct side.

mapping.source_claim_statement: In a Bayesian framework for optimal inference under uncertainty, two qualitatively different types of uncertainty arise: EXPECTED uncertainty from the known unreliability of predictive cues within a stable context, and UNEXPECTED uncertainty from unsignaled context switches that produce strongly inconsistent observations. The authors propose that acetylcholine signals expected uncertainty and norepinephrine signals unexpected uncertainty, with the two neuromodulators interacting both antagonistically and synergistically to enable optimal inference and learning in noisy and changeable environments. The model unifies and explains a wealth of physiological, pharmacological, and behavioural data across attentional cueing, reversal learning, and pharmacological manipulation paradigms.
mapping.ree_translation: Yu & Dayan 2005 anchors the computational distinctness of NE-based signalling. For Q-045 the relevant claim is: the LC-NE substrate (MECH-313 analogue) is doing UNEXPECTED-UNCERTAINTY signalling -- a specific computational primitive (detect context-switch, broadcast policy-belief uncertainty, drive exploration) -- not a generic noise-floor. The dACC anti-recency substrate (MECH-260) is doing something computationally different (recency-weighted bias suppression). The two cannot collapse into one substrate without losing one of the two computational roles. This is theoretical support for the SUBSTRATE-DISTINCT side of Q-045. However, Yu & Dayan's NE role is unexpected-uncertainty signalling, NOT continuous-noise-floor as MECH-313 is currently conceived. The mapping surfaces a potential under-specification of MECH-313: it may need an unexpected-uncertainty trigger to fully match the LC-NE biology that the architectural argument relies on. This is a Phase-2 refinement question, not a current substrate gap.
mapping.mapping_caveat: Theoretical paper, not empirical. The Yu-Dayan framework is a Bayesian-rational model of neuromodulation that the authors validate against existing empirical work but do not independently test. The mapping to REE's MECH-313 is loose: Yu-Dayan describes NE as an unexpected-uncertainty signal, while MECH-313 is currently conceived as an always-on noise floor. These are related but not identical computational primitives. The architectural argument for Q-045 (NE-based and dACC-based substrates are computationally distinct) is robust to this looseness; the implementation refinement (should MECH-313 add an unexpected-uncertainty trigger?) is the question this mapping surfaces. According to PubMed, DOI 10.1016/j.neuron.2005.04.026; PMID 15944135.
mapping.source_context: Theoretical / computational neuroscience review, Neuron 2005. Synthesises Bayesian inference under uncertainty with neuromodulatory pharmacology and attentional cueing behaviour.

confidence_components.notes: Source quality high (Neuron 2005, canonical Bayesian theory of neuromodulation, heavily cited). Mapping fidelity moderate-strong on the substrate-distinctness argument (NE and dACC do computationally different things) but weaker on the direct MECH-313 anchor (Yu-Dayan's NE role is unexpected-uncertainty signalling, not generic noise-floor as MECH-313 currently is). Transfer risk moderate: theoretical-to-empirical-to-substrate, two transfer hops, but the architectural commitment is at the computational-primitive level.
